_note: Causality phrase portfolio (done-line 0103, iterations 0003-0005). Each phrase: cute-on-the-surface, mechanically-rich underneath. glyphs compose facets (type-by-composition, facets from canvas-system.js FACET); relations connect facet→facet across glyphs (refs are 'word' or 'word.facet'); line is a canvas-system LINE type. links stitch membranes by a shared glyph. Mechanics aim to be CORRECT (bdo: the cat/shadow chain is loss-of-warmth → wakes cat, not shadow → cat). A phrase with a genuine commission trap also carries surface_trap {type, edge(from/to/label), why}: the tempting-but-WRONG edge a careless reader draws from the surface — the benchmark teeth (done-line 0105, outcome causality-story-benchmark). trap.type is from recovery_scorer.js TRAP_TYPES; trap.edge must NOT be a true relation and its endpoints must still resolve (a plausible misreading, not nonsense). Omission-style difficulty (e.g. owl-stars' implicit prior) needs no trap — relation-recall already scores it.

phrases.text: The cat naps in the sunbeam until the shadow steals its warmth.
phrases._demo_note: stage = the Complexity-slider level at which a glyph appears (0 = the three-word seed 'The cat naps'); stages.texts = the sentence the slider grows. relations declare (fromGlyph.facet, toGlyph.facet, valence); their label/type/perspective are GENERATED by composeRelation in canvas-system.js (iterations 0008) — no hand-authored label, no construction flag. Additive fields (stage/stages): the benchmark mesh (glyphs/facets/relations) is untouched.

texts: The cat naps in the sunbeam.
The cat naps in the sunbeam until the shadow steals its warmth.

surface_trap.why: the surface 'the shadow steals its warmth' tempts a direct shadow→cat edge; the true mechanism routes through warmth.state — the shadow blocks the warmth, and the fading warmth is what wakes the cat. Recovering the mediator is the test.

phrases.text: The little robot waters the plant when the leaves droop, then checks tomorrow to see if it helped.

surface_trap.why: the surface pairs 'waters' with 'the leaves droop', tempting a direct water→leaves edge; the water actually feeds the plant.state, and it is the plant rising that raises the leaves. The plant is the elided mediator.

phrases.text: The mouse drops a crumb at every turn so the dark can remember the way home.

surface_trap.why: the surface 'so the dark can remember the way home' personifies the dark as the rememberer; the memory lives on the crumb (crumb.memory), not the dark. The dark only hides home — the agency is misattributed.

phrases.text: The owl counts the stars and hoots when one goes missing, and the whole village looks up.
The caterpillar eats one leaf, then asks the branch for another, and the branch points back to the tree.
The bee visits every flower that remembers the sun, and leaves a little gold behind.

surface_trap.why: 'leaves a little gold behind' reads like the bee's reward (honey), tempting a gold→bee edge; the causality runs the other way — the bee leaves pollen (gold) that becomes more flowers (gold.memory→flower.source). The gain is the flower's, not the bee's.

phrases.text: The fox buries a berry for a winter it has never met, and forgets where.

edge.label: the fox knows where it buried

surface_trap.why: the default assumption is that an actor remembers its own action, tempting a fox→where 'remembers' edge; the story inverts it — 'and forgets where' means the location is forgotten BY the fox (where.memory→fox.memory). The trap is the un-inverted assumption.

phrases.text: The moth follows the porch light until morning dissolves it.
